neutralization_fail.signature: barra_ind_size->barra_l3 switch causes step2 ind_corr>0.4, size_corr>0.1
neutralization_fail.fix: Switching from barra_ind_size (with OLS neutralization) to barra_l3 (without) can turn a backtest_fail into a neutralization_fail when the factor concept has inherent industry/size clustering. Volume-based and momentum-based signals often exhibit strong sector biases. The neutralization was correctly masking these biases; removing it does not improve the signal — it just exposes the bias to step2 checks. Always verify whether a factor's cross-sectional IC survives neutralization before assuming the neutralization is the problem.

backtest_fail.signature: AR(1) coefficient * momentum/reversal signal: excellent ICIR (3.46) but invariant structural drawdown (-48% to -52%) during 2017-2018 regime across all parameter combos
backtest_fail.fix: Do not use daily AR(1) autocorrelation as a regime-switching proxy in A-shares. Daily return autocorrelation has negligible cross-sectional variance and cannot distinguish momentum vs reversal phases. Pivot to volatility-adjusted or volume-confirmed reversal mechanisms.

backtest_fail.pattern: LS-alpha trap with long-only beta toxicity
backtest_fail.signature: High ICIR + excellent decile LS Sharpe, but long-only Sharpe < 0.1 and MDD > -40%
backtest_fail.fix: Do not try to rescue with demeaning, vol-scaling, or parameter tuning. If the alpha cannot be extracted long-only after 3 rounds, abandon and pivot to a different behavioral mechanism (e.g., MAX effect, volume-confirmed reversal) that has independent alpha generation.

backtest_fail.signature: ICIR > 2.0 and monotonicity > 0.9, but MDD consistently -43% to -45% across all parameter combos due to a multi-year regime (e.g. 2017-2018) where the factor is directionally wrong. Drawdown start/trough dates are invariant to top_k, decay, or rebalance frequency.
backtest_fail.fix: Do not waste rounds on parameter tuning. If the structural drawdown period is documented in source research (e.g. 华泰 2018 '2017年后回撤较大'), abandon immediately. Record the anti-pattern and pivot to a different behavioral mechanism (e.g. outflow acceleration, net flow volatility, or buy/sell imbalance) rather than raw outflow ratio.

backtest_fail.signature: Pure BP factor: ICIR>1.9, monotonicity>0.9, but Calmar<0.35 due to invariant 2017-2018 drawdown (-38% to -41%) across all strategy params, weak profit filters, and momentum overlays
backtest_fail.fix: Do not waste rounds on parameter tuning or weak quality filters for pure BP. The 2017-2018 'beautiful 50' regime is a structural risk for pure value exposure in A-shares. Abandon immediately and pivot to quality-value composite (BP+ROE from income_q) or a different behavioral mechanism.

backtest_fail.signature: [f_auto_dp_value_001] Pure DP factor: ICIR>1.8, monotonicity>0.9, Sharpe>0.55, but Calmar consistently <0.4 across all strategy params (decay/top_k/rebalance) due to invariant 2017-2018 drawdown (-34% to -35%). Drawdown dates are completely invariant to parameter changes.
backtest_fail.fix: Do not waste rounds on parameter tuning for pure DP. The 2017-2018 'beautiful 50' regime structurally hurts dividend-yield stocks. Quality-overlay via DP×ROE destroys predictive power (raw IC drops 23%, pos_ratio drops from 60% to 54%). Consider DP only as a component in a multi-factor composite (e.g., Barra Value = EP+BP+SP+DP), not as a standalone alpha factor.

backtest_fail.signature: [f_auto_htvp_001] Price-volume correlation factor: ICIR>3.0, monotonicity>0.98, simple_sharpe>0.65, but detailed Calmar consistently 0.27-0.33 across decay=5/10/15 due to structural drawdown >35%. Long-only cannot extract alpha despite excellent IC. Drawdown periods shift with decay but MDD remains >35%.
backtest_fail.fix: Do not waste rounds on parameter tuning for price-volume correlation factors in A-shares. The strong IC comes primarily from short-side alpha (volume-price convergence predicting poor returns), while long-side (divergence) has weak discriminatory power. The factor exhibits 'bear market strong, bull market weak' regime dependence documented in source research. Pivot to volume-confirmed reversal (e.g., turnover_rate spike + price decline) or MAX effect instead.

backtest_fail.signature: [f_auto_20260606_001] Intraday price-pattern factor with ICIR>7.0, monotonicity>0.99, simple_sharpe>0.5, simple_calmar>0.4, but detailed turnover>130% and cost_drag>14% completely erases alpha. Alpha is concentrated in short side (strong negative predictive power) while long side is weak. Short window + 1D rebalance required to extract signal, which causes explosive turnover.
backtest_fail.fix: Do not try to rescue by adjusting decay or rebalance frequency. If a factor requires >100% annual turnover to extract its alpha in detailed backtest, the alpha is not economically viable for long-only A-share portfolios. Abandon after verifying the short-side dominance pattern (check decile group 1 vs group 10 returns). Pivot to factors with balanced long/short decile spreads or fundamentally different behavioral mechanisms.

backtest_fail.signature: Negative chip_profit reversal using market_daily only; best simple variant top50_1W_d3 passes static/simple with Calmar around 0.41, but detailed Calmar collapses near 0.26 with annual_turnover around 29, cost_drag around 5-6%, and fees around one-third of initial capital over 10 years; slower 2W rebalance materially worsens simple Calmar, while larger topN dilutes signal.
backtest_fail.fix: Do not rescue by lowering thresholds or by minor holding-period/topN/window tuning. Only revisit with a materially new formula that neutralizes crash-beta/liquidity exposure and reduces turnover without relying on slower execution that destroys alpha.

backtest_fail.signature: [f_auto_20260607_001] BRAIN-style -ts_sum(low,7)*ts_corr(volume,vwap,3) with ICIR>3.7 but detailed turnover>130% and cost_drag>14% erases all alpha. Decay 9->15 only reduces turnover 171%->135%. Raw low and raw volume expose size bias; when normalized (adj_low+pct_change+turnover_rate), ICIR collapses to 1.51.
backtest_fail.fix: Do not try to rescue by adjusting decay or rebalance frequency. The strong ICIR comes from size exposure (raw low absolute price + raw volume), not genuine behavioral alpha. When normalized, the signal loses predictive power. Pivot to volume-confirmed reversal using stable cross-sectional turnover rank or turnover spike + price decline patterns.

backtest_fail.signature: [f_auto_20260607_001] Panic-selling reversal (-ret_window * vol_ratio) with excellent ICIR (3.34-3.55) and monotonicity (0.81-0.92) but structural drawdown from 2016-11 to 2018-10 (-48% to -53%) invariant across ret_window=7/20/60. Calmar remains 0.14-0.21, far below threshold 0.4.
backtest_fail.fix: Do not waste rounds on window tuning for panic-selling reversal in A-shares. The 2016-2018 'beautiful 50' regime structurally hurts small-cap rebound strategies. The factor works in bear-market rebounds but fails in prolonged small-cap bear regimes. Pivot to MAX effect or turnover-volatility signals that do not depend on mean-reversion after drawdowns.

backtest_fail.signature: [f_auto_20260607_ocrev_001] cs_rank(open / close - 1) shows strong ICIR=3.66, positive ratio=0.61, monotonicity=0.78, and passes simple backtest after shortening decay, but detailed backtest collapses because 1D execution turnover reaches 299x annualized and fees exceed 144% of initial capital. The alpha is too short-lived for long-only A-share execution under realistic costs.
backtest_fail.fix: Do not lower thresholds or keep shortening decay to improve pre-cost metrics. Verify decile long-side versus short-side contribution; if long-side alpha is weak or executable turnover remains above 100x, abandon this intraday reversal formula family for long-only portfolios. Pivot to lower-turnover behavioral or fundamental mechanisms with persistent alpha.

backtest_fail.signature: [f_auto_20260607_004] sigmoid(-ts_corr(vwap/high, high, 15)^2 * log(volume)) produced annual_icir=3.47 and simple_sharpe=0.571, but detailed_sharpe collapsed to 0.026 because annual_turnover=147.6 and cost_drag=13.8% erased the 14.4% simple annual return.
backtest_fail.fix: Do not rescue with cosmetic decay/top_k/rebalance tuning. Replace raw volume and fast VWAP/high rolling-correlation mechanics with stable turnover_rate volatility ranks or slow PIT quality-enhanced turnover signals.

backtest_fail.signature: ts_corr(vwap/high, adjusted close, 10) can show excellent ICIR and monotonicity but fail detailed long-only backtest because annual_turnover exceeds 130% and cost_drag consumes most simple alpha.
backtest_fail.fix: Abandon raw fast VWAP/high rolling-correlation mechanics; pivot to stable turnover_rate volatility ranks or turnover spike plus price-decline reversal patterns rather than tuning decay/top_k/rebalance.

backtest_fail.signature: Raw (vwap-close)*volume can show very high ICIR and pass simple backtest after diversification, but detailed turnover above 200x and cost_drag above 20% erase alpha; simple-to-detailed return gap approximately equals cost_drag.
backtest_fail.fix: Abandon raw intraday VWAP-close volume dislocation for executable long-only portfolios; pivot to slower turnover_rate stability or turnover-spike-plus-price-decline patterns rather than tuning decay/top_k/rebalance.

config_error.signature: Fundamental factor (using income_q/balancesheet_q/cashflow_q) with rebalance frequency shorter than 1M (e.g., 1D/5D/1W/2W)
config_error.fix: Fundamental data updates quarterly; high-frequency rebalancing provides no incremental signal while increasing turnover and costs. Default to rebalance='1M' or 'EOM' for fundamental factors. Only deviate if the factor explicitly uses daily market data as the primary signal source.
